title: How Innodata's (INOD) 2025 Revenue Surge and AI Push Could Reframe Its Investment Narrative
text: Simply Wall St's analysis examines Innodata Inc.'s 2025 financial results and strategic pivot toward artificial intelligence. The company reported Q4 sales of US$72.38 million versus US$59.18 million a year earlier and achieved full-year revenues of approximately $251.66 million, representing significant growth from $170.46 million the previous year. The article highlights management's emphasis on progress in generative AI, agentic AI, and physical AI capabilities. A notable development was the launch of the Innodata Career Academy, which achieved 500-plus employees completing mandatory training with a 99% completion rate and maintained turnover below 5%. The investment narrative centers on Innodata's ability to serve as an essential partner for complex AI projects while managing two primary challenges: customer concentration risk and rising operational costs. The analysis projects $350.9 million in 2028 revenue with potential fair value of $93.75 per share, representing a 112% upside. However, analysts caution that margin compression and customer dependency remain critical near-term risks to monitor.
